Title: Coordination of auxin and ethylene biosynthesis by the aminotransferase VAS1

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pyridoxal phosphate (PLP)-dependent aminotransferase; class I/II aminotransferase family
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Auxin and ethylene biosynthesis interface; IPA/IAA pathway and methionine/ACC-associated ethylene pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Auxin biosynthetic IPA branch with coupling to methionine regeneration affecting ACC/ethylene production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the amino group from L-methionine to indole-3-pyruvic acid, converting IPA back to tryptophan while producing KMBA
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with IAA formation by consuming IPA and simultaneously affects precursor availability for ACC/ethylene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedling shoots, including hypocotyl-associated aerial tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: IAA and ethylene coordinate shade-avoidance growth, hypocotyl elongation, petiole elongation, and root growth responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of VAS1 increases free IAA and decreases ACC, restoring shade-induced elongation defects of sav3 and altering ethylene-related root responses
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for balancing auxin and ethylene precursor flux; not established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in seedling shoots by Pvas1::VAS1-GUS; shade-responsive developmental contexts examined
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in shoots of 7-day-old seedlings; analyzed under white light and simulated shade
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS suppressor screen of sav3; positional cloning; multiple vas1 alleles; transgenic complementation/reporter constructs; recombinant enzyme assays; LC-MS/GC-MS metabolite quantification; RT-qPCR; physiological phenotyping under shade and ACC treatments
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S (constitutive) for VAS1-YFP; native VAS1 promoter for VAS1-GUS and VAS1-GFP
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Simulated shade versus white light; ACC treatment in root elongation assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted metabolite profiling by LC-MRM-MS and GC-MS; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VAS1 was identified as the gene mutated in multiple sav3 suppressors. 2. VAS1 encodes a PLP-dependent aminotransferase that preferentially uses methionine as amino donor and IPA as amino acceptor to form tryptophan. 3. Loss of VAS1 elevates IAA and lowers ACC in shoots, showing that VAS1 coordinates auxin and ethylene biosynthesis by redirecting IPA away from IAA production while consuming methionine.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating VAS1 could tune growth-hormone balance and shade-avoidance architecture, but likely has pleiotropic developmental effects because it simultaneously affects auxin and ethylene metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered hypocotyl/petiole elongation and ethylene-dependent root growth; broad hormone-balance pleiotropy expected
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study measures free ACC rather than conjugated ACC forms. VAS1 was isolated as a suppressor of sav3/TAA1 deficiency, indicating function downstream of IPA formation and in parallel with ethylene precursor balance.
